title: Redundancy and dismissal
summary: 9609 AS — redundancy vs dismissal, legal process, and impact on remaining workforce.
learningObjectives: Explain fair process and consultation requirements.
Analyse impact on morale and employer brand.
Evaluate HR responses to workforce reduction.

simpleExplanation.title: When jobs end
simpleExplanation.summary: Redundancy means the job disappears; dismissal means the person is at fault or unable to perform. Both require fair procedure and affect surviving employees.
steps: Redundancy: role no longer required — not employee fault.
Consultation and notice periods required by law.
Survivors may face lower morale and insecurity.

flashcards.back: Job ceases to exist or employer needs fewer workers — not due to individual fault.
Employer ends employment — may be fair (misconduct) or unfair (no proper process).
Discuss plans with affected staff/unions before final decision — legal requirement.
Statutory/contractual payment based on service — softens financial impact.
Remaining staff feel guilt, fear, lower morale and productivity.
Redeployment, reduced hours, natural wastage, early retirement.
Workforce planning surplus may lead to redundancy programme.
Unfair dismissal claims damage finances and reputation (6.1.1).

sections.content: **Redundancy and dismissal** (2.1.4) appears in **restructuring** cases. Always distinguish **redundancy (job gone)** vs **dismissal (person at fault)** and mention **morale of survivors**.

sections.question: Manufacturing firm automates a production line; 40 operatives' roles removed. Analyse HR issues beyond redundancy payments.
sections.solution:
**Process:** **Consult** unions/staff; fair **selection criteria** (not discriminatory); **notice** and **retraining** offers where possible.

**Survivors:** Remaining staff may fear **further cuts** — **communication** from leadership essential; **2.1.5 welfare** and **2.2 motivation** needed.

**Reputation:** Mass redundancy hurts **employer brand** — harder future **recruitment** (2.1.3).

**Alternatives:** **Redeploy** to quality control or logistics if skills transferable — reduces social and legal risk.
